rag_rules: Fix unresolved identifiers in AGREE annexes by ensuring all referenced features or helpers are visible in the same scope or properly declared.
Place component type contracts in valid component type scopes, avoiding implementation-level bindings in type contracts.
generic_rules: Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers within AGREE annexes to avoid type errors.
Ensure assumptions and guarantees reference concrete bounds or named constants for valid range contracts.
Validate that all identifiers in AGREE annexes are either defined in the same scope or explicitly visible in the AADL model.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must declare helper functions or variables in the same scope where they are used.
Component type contracts should not reference subcomponent ports unless represented through valid variables.
anti_patterns: Avoid referencing identifiers that are not visible or declared in the same AGREE annex or AADL scope.
Do not place implementation-level bindings in component type contracts.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about package or property set visibility
